question_latex: What is the primary objective of systems analysis?
option_a: To replace all human employees with computers
option_b: To gather and interpret facts, diagnose problems, and recommend system improvements
option_c: To write all program code for a new software system
option_d: To manage the financial budget of the entire organization
solution_latex: Systems analysis is the process of gathering and interpreting facts, diagnosing problems, and using that information to recommend improvements to a system.

question_latex: Which characteristic of a system ensures that parts work together within the system even if each performs a unique function?
option_a: Interaction
option_b: Organisation
option_c: Integration
solution_latex: Integration is concerned with how a system is tied together, ensuring that parts work together within the system even though each performs a unique function.

question_latex: Which essential element of system input refers to ensuring data is produced at the least cost?
option_a: Accuracy
option_b: Timeliness
option_c: Proper format
solution_latex: Economy is an essential element of inputs, meaning that the data must be produced at the least cost.

question_latex: What defines a deterministic system?
option_a: A system where the occurrence of all events cannot be perfectly predicted
option_b: A system where the occurrence of all events is perfectly predictable
option_c: A system that is completely isolated from environmental influences
option_d: A system that only uses abstract conceptualization
solution_latex: A deterministic system is one in which the occurrence of all events is perfectly predictable; if the system state is known, the next state can be easily predicted.

question_latex: Which information system is designed specifically to assist managers with unstructured or semi-structured decisions?
option_a: Transaction Processing Systems (TPS)
option_b: Management Information Systems (MIS)
option_c: Decision Support System (DSS)
solution_latex: Decision support systems assist managers who must make decisions that are not highly structured, often called unstructured or semi-structured decisions.

question_latex: What are the three aspects of the feasibility study conducted during a preliminary investigation?
option_a: Technical, Economic, and Operational feasibility
option_b: Financial, Legal, and Strategic feasibility
option_c: Hardware, Software, and Network feasibility
solution_latex: There are three aspects in the feasibility study: Technical Feasibility, Economic Feasibility, and Operational Feasibility.

question_latex: What is the primary philosophy behind software testing?
option_a: To prove that the program is error-free
option_b: To demonstrate that the program functions correctly
option_c: To execute the program with the explicit intention of finding errors
option_d: To minimize the need for documentation
solution_latex: Testing is the processing of executing a program with the explicit intention of finding errors, that is, making the program fail.

question_latex: Which category of system maintenance occurs with the highest relative frequency (60%)?
option_a: Corrective
option_b: Adaptive
option_c: Perfective
solution_latex: Perfective maintenance (user enhancement, improved documentation, recording for computational efficiency) occurs at a frequency of 60%.

question_latex: What is the main purpose of Unit Testing?
option_a: To test the entire system interaction
option_b: To test the programs making up a system independently to find errors in coding and logic
option_c: To perform user acceptance testing
option_d: To test for peak load capacity
solution_latex: Unit testing (or program testing) stresses the modules independently of one another to detect errors in coding and logic contained within that module alone.

question_latex: What is an audit trail in a computerized system?
option_a: A list of all software bugs found during testing
option_b: The path which a transaction traces from source documents to summary reports
option_c: A password protection mechanism
option_d: A summary of the hardware maintenance logs
solution_latex: An audit trail is the path which a transaction traces through a data processing system from source documents to summary reports, allowing transactions to be traced through all stages of processing.

question_latex: Which conversion method is considered the safest because the organization can fall back to the old system if problems arise?
option_a: Direct Conversion
option_b: Parallel Systems
option_c: Pilot System
solution_latex: Parallel systems is the safest method because it allows the organization to fall back to the old system without loss of time or money if problems occur with the new one.

question_latex: What is the primary purpose of a post-implementation review?
option_a: To train the remaining staff on the new system
option_b: To determine whether the system is meeting expectations and where improvements are needed
option_c: To provide a final payment to the hardware vendor
solution_latex: A post-implementation review determines how well the system meets expectations, compares performance against pre-defined requirements, and identifies areas for improvement or re-design.

question_latex: What is the primary disadvantage of outright purchase of computer hardware?
option_a: It is the most expensive option in the long run
option_b: Lack of control over equipment change
option_c: Risk of obsolescence and permanent commitment
solution_latex: The primary disadvantages of outright purchase are the risk of technical obsolescence and the permanent commitment to the system.

question_latex: What is typically contained in a 'run book' for computer operations?
option_a: A list of all software source code
option_b: Operator instructions for each program, including error conditions and restart procedures
option_c: A marketing brochure for the software
option_d: The minutes of the last management meeting
solution_latex: The run book is a collection of operator instructions for each program, containing narratives, error conditions, file assignments, and restart procedures.

question_latex: What is the HIPO acronym based on?
option_a: Hardware, Input, Program, Output
option_b: Hierarchy plus Input Process Output
option_c: Human, Interface, Process, Organization
solution_latex: HIPO is an acronym for Hierarchy plus Input Process Output.

question_latex: In a Data Flow Diagram (DFD), what does a square shape typically define?
option_a: A process that converts data
option_b: A data store or repository
option_c: A source (originator) or destination of system data
solution_latex: In a DFD, a square defines a source (originator) or destination of system data.

question_latex: What is the purpose of a data dictionary?
option_a: To store the actual business data
option_b: To define each data element encountered during system analysis and design
option_c: To act as a backup for the main database
option_d: To manage the security passwords of users
solution_latex: A data dictionary defines each term (data element) encountered during the analysis and design of a new system, providing a list of all key terms used to describe data flows and files.

question_latex: Which tool breaks a system or program down into increasingly detailed levels, starting with the system name at the top?
option_a: Data Dictionary
option_b: Visual Table of Contents (VTOC)
option_c: Flowchart
solution_latex: A Visual Table of Contents (VTOC) breaks a system or program down into increasingly detailed levels, with the name of the system at the top and functions/sub-functions at lower levels.

question_latex: Which training method is most economical when the company wants to involve a large number of personnel?
option_a: Vendor-provided training
option_b: Off-site short-term courses
option_c: In-house training
solution_latex: In-house training is often more economical for training a larger number of personnel compared to travel-intensive or vendor-charged off-site options.

question_latex: Why might a designer choose to limit the number of controls in a system?
option_a: To increase the system's cost
option_b: To ensure 100% security at all times
option_c: To prevent excessive controls from adversely affecting system performance
option_d: Controls are always free to implement
solution_latex: There is a trade-off in systems design; too many controls may adversely affect system performance, while lack of control may create chaos.

question_latex: What is the primary role of a systems analyst?
option_a: Only to program the software
option_b: To conduct studies, identify objectives, and determine procedures to achieve organizational needs
option_c: To manage the hardware infrastructure only
option_d: To replace management during system design
solution_latex: A systems analyst conducts studies, identifies activities and objectives, determines procedures, and designs/implements systems to suit organizational needs.

question_latex: Which of the following is a common problem from the user's point of view contributing to the 'software crisis'?
option_a: Lack of developer recognition
option_b: Difficulty in choosing software and ensuring hardware compatibility
option_c: Portability issues between programming languages
solution_latex: Problems at the user's end, such as choosing software from market availability and ensuring hardware compatibility, are significant contributors to the software crisis.

question_latex: What is the goal of the 'Correctness' quality factor?
option_a: To ensure the program is easy to transport
option_b: To measure the effort required to operate the system
option_c: To measure the extent to which a program meets system specifications and user objectives
option_d: To ensure the program can be easily expanded
solution_latex: Correctness refers to the extent to which a program meets system specifications and user objectives.

question_latex: What is the primary difference between syntax errors and logic errors?
option_a: Syntax errors deal with data values, logic errors with language rules
option_b: Syntax errors are caught by compilers, logic errors are not
option_c: Logic errors are caused by hardware malfunctions, syntax errors by software
option_d: Syntax errors occur during runtime, logic errors during compilation
solution_latex: A syntax error violates language rules and is shown by error messages; a logic error deals with incorrect data fields or combinations and is not detected by the compiler.

question_latex: What is the primary advantage of using a Data Centre (Service Bureau) instead of an in-house facility?
option_a: Full control over security of all business data
option_b: Avoiding large initial capital expenditure and staffing problems
option_c: Ensuring the staff becomes familiar with working on computers
option_d: Guaranteed priority over other bureau clients
solution_latex: The major benefit of using a data centre is utilizing computer facilities without spending large initial amounts or managing the recruitment of technical staff.

question_latex: Which of the following is an element of a formal documentation package?
option_a: Manager's private email correspondence
option_b: Cover letter and Table of contents
option_c: The original feasibility meeting agenda only
option_d: A copy of the hardware warranty registration
solution_latex: Elements of a documentation package include a cover letter, table of contents, narrative, flowcharts, file specifications, program specifications, and test brochures.

question_latex: What is the purpose of decomposing a data conversion (circle) in a DFD?
option_a: To make the diagram more complex
option_b: To explain the internal process more thoroughly when too many events occur at one point
option_c: To allow the arrows to cross each other
option_d: To remove the need for naming the circles
solution_latex: If too many events occur at a point, an analyst can decompose a data conversion to form a parent-child relationship, helping to clarify complex processes.

question_latex: Why should a company insist on complete and meaningful program documentation?
option_a: To satisfy legal requirements regarding software ownership
option_b: To ensure continuity of information due to normal personnel turnover
option_c: To discourage programmers from changing jobs
option_d: To reduce the cost of computer memory
solution_latex: Personnel turnover is normal, and complete documentation ensures continuity for future programmers who may need to modify programs.

question_latex: What does a passive data dictionary perform?
option_a: In-line transaction validation and editing
option_b: Documentation only
option_c: Exporting database definitions for execution-time performance
solution_latex: The functionally passive data dictionary performs documentation only, which is a critical service for global organization documentation.

question_latex: What does an IPO chart define?
option_a: The management structure of the IT department
option_b: The inputs, processing, and outputs for each module in the program
option_c: The budget requirements for hardware procurement
option_d: The file storage path on a hard disk
solution_latex: An IPO chart defines the inputs, processing, and outputs for each module in the program, illustrating the logic required to produce desired purposes.

question_latex: What is the primary function of the 'Process' element in a structured flowchart?
option_a: Representing alternative conditions
option_b: Representing simple steps, initialization, input/output operations, or calls to procedures
option_c: Representing looping and repetition
solution_latex: In structured flowcharts, the rectangular process symbol represents simple steps, initialization of values, I/O operations, and calls to other procedures.

question_latex: What is the main definition of 'Corrective Maintenance'?
option_a: Adding new user features
option_b: Repairing processing/performance failures or making alterations due to previously ill-defined problems
option_c: Changing program functions to accommodate hardware upgrades
solution_latex: Corrective maintenance involves repairing processing or performance failures and making alterations to fix ill-defined problems.

question_latex: What does a 'Specific Performance' clause in a computer contract allow?
option_a: The buyer to cancel the contract without penalty
option_b: The buyer to demand additional memory at nil or nominal cost if acceptance tests fail
option_c: The vendor to increase prices based on inflation
option_d: The buyer to return hardware after 5 years
solution_latex: Specific performance clauses are invoked when a system fails acceptance tests due to memory storage; the buyer can ask for additional memory at minimal cost.

question_latex: Why is carpeted flooring generally avoided in a computer room?
option_a: It increases the air conditioning cost
option_b: It is difficult to clean
option_c: Static electricity is very harmful for computers
option_d: It makes it difficult to move heavy equipment
solution_latex: Static electricity is very harmful for computers, so carpets should be avoided in computer rooms; if necessary, only anti-static carpet should be used.

question_latex: Which of the following is NOT an objective of system control?
option_a: Accuracy
option_b: Reliability
option_c: Speed of data entry
solution_latex: The objectives of system control include Accuracy, Reliability, Security, Efficiency, Audit, and Adherence to organization policies.

question_latex: What is the primary responsibility of programmers during the 'Development of Software' stage?
option_a: Defining the initial feasibility of the project
option_b: Installing purchased hardware
option_c: Coding, documenting the program, and explaining procedures
solution_latex: Programmers are responsible for developing custom programs, documenting them, and explaining how and why procedures are coded in specific ways.

question_latex: What is the goal of 'Storage Testing'?
option_a: To determine the length of time used to process data
option_b: To determine the capacity of the system to store transaction data on disk or files
option_c: To ensure users can recover data after a failure
option_d: To test for human factor errors
solution_latex: Storage testing determines the capacity of the system to store transaction data on disks or in other files.

question_latex: What is the 'logical design' of an information system?
option_a: The actual hardware purchase
option_b: The detailed description of how a system will meet the requirements identified during analysis
option_c: The actual physical coding of programs
solution_latex: Systems design produces the details that clearly describe how a system will meet the requirements identified during analysis; this stage is referred to as logical design.

question_latex: What does 'Interaction' refer to in a system?
option_a: The procedure in which each component functions with other components
option_b: The hierarchical structure of management
option_c: The central objective of the system
solution_latex: Interaction refers to the procedure in which each component functions with other components of the system to solve problems.

question_latex: Which of the following is a characteristic of good documentation?
option_a: It should be complex to discourage unauthorized access
option_b: It must be objective and clearly defined in language that is easily understood
option_c: It does not need to be cross-referable
option_d: It should be maintained only once at the end of the project
solution_latex: Documentation should have objectivity, meaning it must be clearly defined in a language that is easily understood by those for whom it is intended.

question_latex: What defines 'External controls' to a system?
option_a: Plans and procedures set by management for internal computer use
option_b: Laws, regulations, procedures, and policies outside the scope of the system
option_c: Password protection within the software
solution_latex: External controls are laws, regulations, procedures, and policies outside the scope of the system which affect its operations.

question_latex: When evaluating vendor bids, what should an analyst do if a vendor fails to meet 'Mandatory requirements'?
option_a: Assign a low weight to the feature
option_b: Ask the vendor to lower their price
option_c: Screen the vendor out without any reason
option_d: Compare the vendor with others on merits
solution_latex: If a vendor fails to meet mandatory requirements, they are screened out immediately without further consideration.

question_latex: What does an active data dictionary do?
option_a: Performs documentation only without computer help
option_b: Supports program and operations development by exporting database definitions
option_c: Runs only in-line during program execution
solution_latex: An active data dictionary supports program and operations development by exporting database definitions and program data storage definitions for languages like COBOL.

question_latex: What is the purpose of 'String Testing'?
option_a: To test the system against peak load levels
option_b: To test whether programs conform to related programs in the total system interaction
option_c: To test the hardware physical stability
solution_latex: String testing ensures that programs interact correctly and conform to related programs in the total system.

question_latex: What is a 'Warnier/Orr diagram' primarily used for?
option_a: Documenting organization charts
option_b: Designing program structures by working backwards from output to input
option_c: Representing hardware layouts
solution_latex: Warnier/Orr diagrams are tools for designing program structures by identifying outputs and processing results and working backwards to determine inputs.

question_latex: What is the primary function of 'Error Tolerance' in a system?
option_a: Preventing errors from occurring
option_b: Recognizing errors and correcting the effect so the system does not fail
option_c: Recognizing errors but keeping the system running through degraded performance
option_d: Shutting down the entire system upon error detection
solution_latex: Error tolerance recognizes errors when they occur but enables the system to keep running, often by shutting down parts of the system or accepting degraded performance.

question_latex: Which of the following is a key to reducing the need for maintenance?
option_a: Writing documentation after the project is completed
option_b: More accurately defining user requirements during systems development
option_c: Hiring fewer programmers
option_d: Avoiding the use of structured design tools
solution_latex: Accurately defining user requirements during the development phase is a key to reducing the need for subsequent maintenance.

question_latex: What is the 'Phase-in' method of conversion?
option_a: Converting everything abruptly over a weekend
option_b: Staging the implementation over a period of time (weeks or months)
option_c: Running old and new systems simultaneously
option_d: Implementing the system in one single department only
solution_latex: The phase-in method involves staging the implementation over a period of time, often due to constraints in file conversion, training, or equipment arrival.

question_latex: What is the primary objective of 'User Acceptance Testing'?
option_a: To test the hardware peak load
option_b: To sell the user on the validity and reliability of the system
option_c: To debug syntax errors in the code
solution_latex: User acceptance testing has the objective of selling the user on the validity and reliability of the system and verifying that procedures operate to specifications.

question_latex: Which symbol in a data dictionary represents 'Either/Or'?
solution_latex: In a data dictionary, the symbol [] represents Either/Or, () represents an optional entry, = is equivalent to, and + means And.

question_latex: What is the purpose of a 'Benchmark' in software evaluation?
option_a: To act as a standard for employee salary
option_b: A sample program specially designed to evaluate comparative performance
option_c: A list of satisfied existing users
option_d: A report from an independent research organization
solution_latex: Benchmarking is a sample program designed to evaluate the comparative performance of hardware or software.

question_latex: What is the most important form of documentation in the system development cycle?
option_a: The initial user verbal request
option_b: The feasibility report
option_c: The programmers' coffee break schedule
solution_latex: A feasibility report is the most important form of documentation as it defines objectives in reasonable detail and provides a plan to attain them.

question_latex: Why is an audit trail particularly important in online systems?
option_a: Because batch systems do not have audit trails
option_b: Because source documents might not exist, and changes might be made without leaving visible clues
option_c: Because auditors are required to be online at all times
option_d: Because it makes the system faster
solution_latex: In online systems, unlike batch environments, there may not be source documents, and users can make changes without leaving visible clues, making an audit trail essential.

question_latex: Which financing option for computer systems requires no cash up front and protects against technical obsolescence?
option_a: Outright Purchase
option_b: Renting
option_c: Leasing
solution_latex: Leasing requires no capital investment (no cash up front) and protects the user against technical obsolescence for the duration of the lease.

question_latex: What do Warnier/Orr diagrams use to indicate each level of a system?
option_a: A series of circles
option_b: A set of brackets
option_c: Arrows and boxes
solution_latex: Warnier/Orr diagrams employ a set of brackets to group components and indicate each level of the system clearly.
